title: 10 best herbal creams for abdominal pain

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as apigenin and luteolin, help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can alleviate abdominal pain caused by conditions like irritable bowel syndrome (IBS).

Antispasmodic properties: Chamomile's antispasmodic effects help relax muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramps that can cause abdominal pain, such as those experienced during menstrual periods or due to food allergies.

Anxiolytic and sedative properties: Chamomile's anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) and sedative properties can help soothe anxiety-induced stomach issues, such as bloating, cramps, and diarrhea, which are common symptoms of stress-related digestive disorders.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Matricaria chamomilla, also known as chamomile, creams helps with abdominal pain because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The cream's active compounds, such as apigenin and luteolin, have been shown to reduce inflammation and relax smooth muscle in the digestive tract. This can help alleviate cramps, spasms, and discomfort associated with menstrual cramps, irritable bowel syndrome, and other abdominal issues. The calming effects of chamomile also promote relaxation, further reducing abdominal pain and discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Apigenin: This flavonoid helps reduce abdominal pain by acting as a potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant agent, thereby alleviating inflammation and discomfort in the abdominal region.
Luteolin: Luteolin, a flavonoid and phenolic compound, exhibits analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which contribute to its ability to alleviate abdominal pain and discomfort.
α-Bisabolol: This sesquiterpene has anti-inflammatory and soothing effects, helping to reduce inflammation and calm the abdominal muscles, thereby providing relief from abdominal pain and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers are rich in apigenin and other flavonoids that have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, making them effective in reducing abdominal pain and inflammation.
Leaves: The leaves contain essential oils and flavonoids that have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, helping to relax the muscles and reduce abdominal cramps.
Seeds: The seeds contain sesquiterpene lactones that have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, helping to reduce inflammation and alleviate abdominal pain.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 50g of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Heat 100g of distilled water to 90°c and then infuse it with the matricaria chamomilla powder for 10 minutes.
Strain the infused mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container.
Mix 20g of beeswax and 20g of candelilla wax with 50g of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Combine the cooled infused mixture with the melted wax mixture and stir until it thickens into a smooth cream.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Jesuit's Bark (Cinchona officinalis) cream contains quinine, which has anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce swelling and alleviate abdominal pain caused by inflammation.

Antispasmodic properties: The alkaloids present in Jesuit's Bark, such as quinine and cinchonidine, have antispasmodic effects that can relax smooth muscles and relieve cramps, spasms, and other forms of abdominal pain.

Pain-relieving properties: Quinine and other compounds in Jesuit's Bark have analgesic and anesthetic properties, which can help numb the area affected by abdominal pain, reducing discomfort and increasing mobility.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cinchona officinalis, also known as Jesuit's bark, creams helps with abdominal pain because of its unique properties. The cream contains quinine, a natural compound extracted from the plant, which has anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects. These properties help to reduce inflammation and ease pain in the abdominal area, providing relief from cramps, spasms, and other discomforts. The natural ingredients in Cinchona officinalis cream work harmoniously to soothe and calm the abdominal muscles, promoting a sense of relaxation and comfort.
remedy_constituents: Quinidine: A quinidine is an alkaloid that helps reduce inflammation and relieve pain by blocking the action of acetylcholine, a neurotransmitter involved in the transmission of pain signals.
Quinine: Quinine is an alkaloid that acts as a natural anti-inflammatory agent and analgesic, helping to reduce pain and discomfort associated with abdominal conditions such as cramping and spasms.

Quinic Acid: Quinic acid is a phenolic compound that has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help alleviate abdominal pain by reducing inflammation and muscle contractions.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in quinine, a compound that helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with abdominal pain.
Barks: The barks of Cinchona officinalis contain alkaloids like quinine and quinidine, which have anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties.

Roots: The roots of Cinchona officinalis contain a range of bioactive compounds, including alkaloids and glycosides, that may contribute to their potential pain-relieving and anti-inflammatory effects.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 20 grams of dried cinchona officinalis bark in 500 milliliters of carrier oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the infusion through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container discarding the solids.
Combine 50 grams of beeswax and 20 grams of shea butter in a double boiler.
Add 20 milliliters of the infused oil to the melted beeswax and shea butter mixture.
Pour the mixture into a mold and allow it to set at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Lemon balm's antispasmodic properties help to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing cramps and spasms that can cause abdominal pain.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Lemon balm's anti-inflammatory compounds help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can contribute to abdominal pain and discomfort.
Antioxidant properties: Lemon balm's antioxidant compounds help protect the digestive tract from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis, also known as lemon balm, creams helps with abdominal pain because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The active compounds in lemon balm, such as rosmarinic acid, have a calming effect on the digestive system, reducing inflammation and spasms that can cause abdominal discomfort. Additionally, lemon balm's soothing properties may help to relax the muscles in the abdominal area, providing relief from cramps, spasms, and other painful sensations associated with digestive issues. This natural remedy may help to alleviate abdominal pain in a gentle and non-invasive way.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic compound exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help alleviate abdominal pain by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the digestive tract.
Linalool: As a terpene, linalool has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help mitigate abdominal pain by reducing muscle spasms and promoting relaxation in the abdominal region.

Limonene: This terpene has been shown to have antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate abdominal pain by relaxing smooth muscle contractions and reducing inflammation in the digestive tract.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used for their anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which help to soothe and calm abdominal cramps.
Roots: They contain compounds with anti-inflammatory and carminative properties, which help to ease abdominal pain and discomfort.
Stems: They are used for their antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to relax the muscles and reduce inflammation in the abdominal area.
remedy_recipe: Harvest melissa officinalis leaves in the morning after the dew has dried to ensure optimal potency and freshness.
Dry the leaves in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours or in a dehydrator for 6 hours.
Infuse the dried melissa officinalis leaves in a carrier oil such as sweet almond oil at a ratio of 1:5 for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean container discarding the solids and store in a cool dark place for up to 6 months.
Mix the infused oil with beeswax at a ratio of 1:1 to create a solid cream base and heat in a double boiler until melted.

remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Herbal Maidenhair Tree Creams contain compounds that help relax smooth muscle spasms in the digestive tract, reducing cramping and discomfort associated with abdominal pain.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The creams' anti-inflammatory effects can reduce inflammation in the gut, which may contribute to abdominal pain and discomfort, while also soothing irritated tissues.

Antioxidant properties: Herbal Maidenhair Tree Creams contain antioxidants that help protect against oxidative stress and cellular damage, which can contribute to chronic abdominal pain and digestive issues.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba, also known as maidenhair tree, creams helps with abdominal pain because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The cream's active compounds, such as flavonoids and terpenoids, work to reduce inflammation and alleviate discomfort in the abdominal area. Ginkgo biloba creams have been traditionally used to soothe digestive issues and relieve cramps, making them a popular natural remedy for abdominal pain. This natural approach may also help to promote relaxation and reduce stress, contributing to overall digestive well-being.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds help alleviate abdominal pain by reducing inflammation and improving blood flow to the affected area, which may help alleviate spasms and cramps.
Bilobalide: This sesquiterpene lactone has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the abdominal region, contributing to pain relief.

Quercetin: As a potent flavonoid and anti-inflammatory agent, quercetin may help reduce abdominal pain by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines, which can contribute to pain and inflammation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part of Ginkgo biloba to make creams for abdominal pain due to their rich flavonoid and terpene content.
Seeds: Ginkgo seeds are also used to make creams for abdominal pain, as they contain ginkgolic acid, which has anti-inflammatory properties.
Barks: The bark of Ginkgo biloba is sometimes used in creams for abdominal pain due to its content of bilobalide, a compound that may help reduce pain and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Weigh and combine 100 grams of ginkgo biloba extract with 50 grams of shea butter in a mixing bowl.
Mix the ginkgo biloba extract and shea butter mixture with 20 grams of beeswax pellets until well combined.
Add 20 milliliters of jojoba oil and 10 milliliters of vitamin e oil to the mixture and stir until smooth.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 160 degrees fahrenheit for 10 minutes to melt the beeswax.
Pour the warm mixture into a clean container and let it cool and solidify at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Kansas coneflower creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like triterpenoid saponins, which help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate abdominal pain.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in Kansas coneflower creams, such as phenolic acids and flavonoids, help neutralize free radicals that can contribute to abdominal pain and discomfort.

Softenening mucous membrane effects: Some studies suggest that Kansas coneflower creams may have a softening effect on the mucous membranes in the digestive tract, which can help reduce inflammation, swelling, and abdominal pain associated with conditions like irritable bowel syndrome (IBS).
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Echinacea angustifolia, also known as Kansas coneflower, creams helps with abdominal pain because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The cream's active compounds, such as alkylamides and caffeic acid, work to reduce swelling and ease discomfort in the abdominal area. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to treat digestive issues, including irritable bowel syndrome and menstrual cramps. By applying the cream topically, individuals can experience fast and effective relief from abdominal pain without the need for medication or invasive treatments.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: These compounds help with abdominal pain by reducing inflammation and modulating the immune response, potentially alleviating symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) and other abdominal conditions.

Alkaloids (e.g., echinacoside): Echinacoside has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that may help mitigate abdominal pain by reducing inflammation, infection, and irritation in the digestive tract.

Terpenoids (e.g., echinulin): Echinulin has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that could contribute to pain relief by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the abdominal region, thereby alleviating discomfort associated with conditions like IBS.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in compounds such as alkylamides, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate abdominal pain.
Leaves: Contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that exhibit analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, providing relief from abdominal discomfort.
Seeds: Contain isobutylamides, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that can help soothe abdominal pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 pound of dried roots from echinacea angustifolia plants in the fall after flowering.
Clean the dried roots by gently stirring them in a mixture of 1 cup water and 1 tablespoon vinegar for 10 minutes.
Steam the roots by submerging them in a double boiler and maintaining a temperature of 100-150 degrees fahrenheit for 30 minutes.
Infuse the steamed roots by steeping them in a mixture of 1 quart water and 1 ounce dried roots for 4-6 hours.
Emulsify the infused liquid by blending it with 1/2 cup beeswax and 1/4 cup coconut oil for 10 minutes then whipping until creamy.

remedy_properties: Hyperforin: This compound has potent anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce inflammation and alleviate abdominal pain caused by conditions such as irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) or inflammatory bowel disease (IBD).

Hypotensive properties: St John's Wort creams have been shown to decrease blood pressure in the pelvic region, which can help relieve menstrual cramps, dysmenorrhea, and other types of abdominal pain associated with hormonal fluctuations.

Antispasmodic effects: The compounds present in St John's Wort creams can help relax smooth muscle tissue in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramping that can cause abdominal pain due to conditions like IBS or constipation.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum, also known as St John's Wort, creams helps with abdominal pain because of its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. The active compounds in Hypericum perforatum, such as hyperforin and hypericin, have been shown to reduce inflammation and relax smooth muscle in the abdominal region, providing relief from cramps and discomfort. Additionally, its analgesic properties help to numb the pain, providing quick and effective relief from abdominal pain, making it a popular natural remedy for digestive issues.
remedy_constituents: Hypericin: This naphthodianthrone derivative has anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce inflammation and alleviate abdominal pain.
Hyperforin: This phenolic compound has analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, which help to block pain signals and reduce inflammation in the abdominal region.

Flavonoids (including rutin and quercetin): These plant-derived compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce inflammation, alleviate pain, and protect the abdominal tissues from oxidative damage.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in hyperforin and other compounds that help reduce inflammation and relieve abdominal pain.
Flowers: Contain hyperforin and other bioactive compounds that have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Stems: Contain a mixture of hyperforin and other compounds that help reduce inflammation and alleviate abdominal pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh hypericum perforatum flowers and leaves in the early morning to ensure optimal potency.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm place with good air circulation for 2 weeks.
Combine 20 grams of dried hypericum perforatum with 50 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 2 hours then strain the liquid through a cheesecloth.
Mix the resulting oil with 10 grams of beeswax and 10 grams of shea butter then whip until smooth.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Maypop cream's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce swelling and irritation in the abdomen, providing relief from painful conditions such as irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) and menstrual cramps.

Antispasmodic properties: The antispasmodic compounds in maypop cream relax muscles in the abdominal wall, easing spasms and cramps associated with conditions like IBS, diverticulitis, and gallstones.
Anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) properties: Maypop cream's anxiolytic compounds help calm the mind and body, reducing stress and anxiety that can exacerbate abdominal pain and discomfort.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Passiflora incarnata, also known as maypop, creams helps with abdominal pain because of its calming and antispasmodic properties. The natural compounds in Passiflora incarnata, such as flavonoids and alkaloids, have been shown to reduce inflammation and relax smooth muscle tissue in the abdominal region, providing relief from cramping and discomfort. Additionally, the creams may help to reduce anxiety and stress, which can often contribute to abdominal pain, promoting overall relaxation and well-being.
remedy_constituents: Harmane: Harmane is a beta-carboline alkaloid that has been shown to have a sedative effect, which can help alleviate abdominal pain by reducing muscle spasms and promoting relaxation.
Isophytol: Isophytol is a diterpene that has been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help reduce inflammation and ease abdominal pain.

Isovitexin: Isovitexin is a flavonoid glycoside that has been demonstrated to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help alleviate abdominal pain by reducing inflammation and pain perception.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their sedative and anti-inflammatory properties.
Fruits: They are used because they contain various alkaloids and flavonoids that help in reducing pain and inflammation.
Roots: They are used for their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help in soothing abdominal pain.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 1 cup of fresh passiflora incarnata flowers in 2 cups of carrier oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container.
Combine 1/4 cup of the infused oil with 1/4 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of coconut oil.
Heat the mixture over low heat, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax is fully melted in 15 minutes.
Pour the mixture into small tin containers and let cool to create a smooth cream in 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as aloin and aloe-emodin, help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can alleviate abdominal pain caused by conditions like irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) or inflammatory bowel disease (IBD).

Soothing gel polysaccharides: The gel-like substance found in aloe vera contains polysaccharides that help soothe and calm irritated mucous membranes in the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with abdominal pain.

Antioxidant activity: Aloe vera's antioxidant properties can help neutralize free radicals that can contribute to oxidative stress and tissue damage in the digestive system, which may exacerbate abdominal pain caused by conditions like gastritis or ulcerative colitis.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe vera, also known as aloe, creams helps with abdominal pain because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The gel extracted from aloe vera plants contains compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and soothe digestive issues. Aloe vera creams can help to calm irritated tissues, relax the digestive system, and reduce spasms that may be contributing to abdominal pain. By promoting a healthy balance of gut bacteria and reducing inflammation, aloe vera creams can provide relief from abdominal discomfort and promote overall digestive well-being.
remedy_constituents: Anthraquinones: These glycosides help with abdominal pain by reducing inflammation and modulating gut motility, thus alleviating symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) and other gastrointestinal disorders.

Phenolic acids (specifically, ferulic acid): These compounds exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help mitigate abdominal pain by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the gastrointestinal tract.

Saponins: These triterpenoid glycosides help with abdominal pain by exerting anti-inflammatory and anti-spasmodic effects, thereby relaxing smooth muscle and reducing pain and discomfort in the abdominal region.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The most commonly used part of Aloe vera for creams, as they contain high amounts of aloe-emodin, a compound that helps soothe and calm abdominal pain.
Gel from Leaves: The clear gel extracted from the inner part of the Aloe vera leaves contains anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and ease abdominal discomfort.
Juice from Leaves: The juice extracted from the leaves has been traditionally used to treat various digestive issues, including abdominal pain, due to its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 100g of aloe vera gel and 50g of beeswax into a double boiler.
Melt the beeswax in 50g of coconut oil over low heat for 5 minutes.
Combine the aloe vera gel with 10g of vitamin e oil and 5g of glycerin in a bowl.
Gradually add the melted beeswax mixture to the aloe vera mixture and stir until fully incorporated.
Pour the mixture into a tin and let it cool and set for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Cape Jasmine cream has anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with abdominal pain, such as cramps or spasms.
Antispasmodic properties: The antispasmodic effects of Cape Jasmine cream help to relax the muscles in the abdominal area, relieving spasms and cramps that can cause pain.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in Cape Jasmine cream help neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which can contribute to abdominal pain.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Gardenia jasminoides, also known as cape jasmine, creams helps with abdominal pain because of its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. The creams contain compounds like geniposide and gardenoside, which have been traditionally used to soothe digestive issues. These compounds help to relax the muscles in the abdominal region, reducing cramps and spasms that can cause discomfort and pain. remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant-derived compounds help alleviate abdominal pain by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the abdominal region.

Glycosides: Specifically, gardenoside, a type of glycoside found in Gardenia jasminoides, has been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help to reduce pain and inflammation in the abdominal area.
Tannins: These polyphenolic compounds have been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory and astringent properties, which help to reduce inflammation and alleviate abdominal pain.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their antispasmodic properties which can help relieve abdominal cramps.
Flowers: They contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory and soothing effects, which can help alleviate abdominal pain.
Leaves: (second instance) They also have a calming effect and are used to make creams for soothing and relaxing abdominal muscles.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-15 fresh gardenia jasminoides flowers with long stems and no signs of wilting.
Steep 1 tablespoon of dried flowers in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the liquid and discard the solids then mix in 2 tablespoons of glycerin.
Combine 1 tablespoon of beeswax with 2 tablespoons of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Blend the infused liquid with the melted wax and oil mixture to create a smooth consistency.

remedy_properties: Sedative and Anti-Anxiety Properties: Valerian's sedative and anti-anxiety effects can help reduce abdominal pain caused by stress, anxiety, or digestive issues like IBS.
Spasmolytic Properties: Valerian's ability to relax smooth muscle tissue can help alleviate cramps, spasms, and contractions in the intestines and abdominal walls that contribute to abdominal pain.
Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Valerian's anti-inflammatory properties can reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which may be a contributing factor to abdominal pain and discomfort.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis, also known as valerian, creams helps with abdominal pain because they contain valerenic acid, a natural compound that interacts with the body's GABA receptors. This interaction has a calming effect on the nervous system, which in turn can help alleviate muscle spasms and cramps that often contribute to abdominal pain. Additionally, valerian creams have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and discomfort in the abdominal area, providing relief from painful cramps and spasms.
remedy_constituents: Isovaleric acid: This organic acid derived from valerenic acid has sedative and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce abdominal pain by reducing muscle spasms and inflammation.

Valerenic acid: A sesquiterpene responsible for Valeriana officinalis's sedative properties, valerenic acid helps alleviate abdominal pain by promoting relaxation, reducing anxiety, and modulating the nervous system.

Valeranone: A sesquiterpene lactone with sedative and anti-inflammatory properties, valeranone helps in reducing abdominal pain by relaxing the muscles, decreasing inflammation, and improving sleep quality.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Valeriana officinalis are primarily used due to their high concentration of valerenic acid, which has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
Stems: The stems of Valeriana officinalis are also used, as they contain a smaller but still significant amount of valerenic acid, contributing to their pain-relieving effects.

Leaves: The leaves of Valeriana officinalis are sometimes used in combination with other parts, as they contain a milder but still beneficial amount of valerenic acid, which may help soothe and calm abdominal discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest valeriana roots and clean them thoroughly in cold running water to remove dirt and debris.
Dry the cleaned valeriana roots in a warm dry place or using a food dehydrator at 50c for 24 hours.
Combine dried valeriana roots with 100g of beeswax and 100g of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture at 60c for 1 hour while stirring occasionally to ensure even melting of the ingredients.
Strain the cream through a cheesecloth and store it in an airtight container in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Beach rose creams helps with abdominal pain because of its unique blend of herbs that work synergistically to soothe and calm the digestive system. The anti-inflammatory properties of the herbal extracts help to reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, while the calming agents promote relaxation and ease cramping. Additionally, the moisturizing effects of the cream help to nourish and protect the skin around the abdomen, providing a sense of comfort and relief from discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Herbal beach rose creams containing ingredients like salicylic acid can help reduce inflammation in the abdomen, which can alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) and other inflammatory conditions that cause abdominal pain.

Antioxidant properties: Antioxidants present in herbal beach rose creams, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, can help protect the digestive tract from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which may contribute to abdominal pain and discomfort.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 250g of dried rosa rugosa petals and 50g of calendula flowers in late summer for optimal potency.
Combine the dried herbs in a heat-proof bowl with 200ml of sweet almond oil and 100ml of beeswax pellets in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture at 70-80°c for 30 minutes while stirring occasionally to infuse the oils with herbal properties.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean glass jar discarding the solids after 10-15 minutes to obtain the herbal extract.
Mix the herbal extract with 100ml of coconut oil and 20ml of aloe vera gel in a hand blender until smooth for final preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel creams helps with abdominal pain because they contain a natural anti-inflammatory compound called anethole, which soothes the digestive tract and relieves cramps, spasms, and discomfort. Additionally, fennel's carminative properties help to eliminate gas and bloating, reducing the pressure and tension that can contribute to abdominal pain. The creamy texture of fennel creams also provides a soothing, gentle coating to the stomach lining, providing further relief from irritation and inflammation.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Fennel creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like anethole, which help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), such as cramps and discomfort.

Carminative properties: The volatile oils present in fennel cream, particularly anise oil, have carminative properties that help release trapped gas and relieve bloating and flatulence associated with abdominal pain.

Muscle relaxant effects: Fennel's flavonoids and terpenes have been shown to possess muscle relaxant properties, which can help soothe cramps, spasms, and discomfort in the abdominal muscles, providing relief from abdominal pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Fennel roots have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in relieving abdominal pain and reducing inflammation.
Seeds: Fennel seeds contain anethole, a natural oil with analgesic and antispasmodic properties, which helps to soothe and calm abdominal cramps.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh foeniculum vulgare leaves and stems in the morning after dew has dried.
Dry the harvested foeniculum vulgare plant material using air circulation or machine drying method.
Combine 100 grams of dried foeniculum vulgare with 500 milliliters of cold pressed sweet almond oil in a glass container.
Heat the mixture to 60°c over low heat for two hours then steep for an additional four hours.
Strain and filter the infused oil through cheesecloth or coffee filter and transfer to sterilized jars for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion creams helps with abdominal pain because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The creams contain extracts from the dandelion plant, which has been traditionally used to relieve digestive issues such as constipation, diarrhea, and bloating. When applied topically, the creams can help reduce inflammation and irritation in the abdominal area, providing fast relief from discomfort and pain. Additionally, the creams' antimicrobial properties can also help prevent infections that may contribute to abdominal pain.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Dandelion cream contains anti-inflammatory compounds such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, alleviating abdominal pain and discomfort.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in dandelion cream, including vitamins A, C, and E, help neutralize free radicals that can contribute to oxidative stress and damage in the body, thereby reducing the risk of abdominal pain and discomfort.

Soothing and protective effects on gut mucosa: Dandelion's saponins and other compounds have been shown to soothe and protect the gut mucosa, which can become inflamed or irritated due to conditions such as IBS, Crohn's disease, or ulcerative colitis, leading to abdominal pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, making them effective in reducing abdominal cramps and pain.
Seeds: The seeds contain mucilage, which helps soothe and protect the digestive tract lining, alleviating symptoms of abdominal discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh dandelion roots and leaves according to botanical standards ensuring quality control measures.
Dry the collected plant material using shade drying methods taking approximately 7-10 days.
Grind the dried plant material into a fine powder using a mill or mortar and pestle method.
Mix 250ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond or coconut oil with 25g of the ground herbal powder and stir well.
Allow the mixture to infuse for 2 weeks at room temperature under gentle agitation before straining and whipping into a creamy texture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild indigo creams helps with abdominal pain because they contain the herb Baptisia tinctoria, which has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. When applied topically to the abdomen, the cream can help soothe irritated tissues, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), such as cramping, bloating, and diarrhea. The cream's natural ingredients also help regulate digestion and restore balance to the gut microbiome, providing relief from abdominal pain and discomfort.

Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial properties of wild indigo cream can help combat bacterial and fungal infections that may be contributing to abdominal pain, such as diverticulitis or appendicitis.

Anesthetic: Wild indigo cream's anesthetic properties can help numb the affected area, providing fast relief from acute abdominal pain caused by conditions like gastritis, ulcers, or menstrual cramps.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe and calm abdominal cramps and discomfort.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are employed because they contain a compound called berberine, which has natural analgesic and antispasmodic effects, making it effective in reducing abdominal pain.
remedy_recipe: Infuse dried baptisia tinctoria roots in cold-pressed sweet almond oil at a ratio of 10% herb to 90% oil for 2 hours.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth and discard the solids after 4 weeks.
Mix the infused oil with beeswax and candelilla wax in a double boiler, heating until melted at 180°f.
Remove from heat and let cool to 140°f before adding vitamin e oil and aloe vera gel, stirring well to combine.
Pour the mixture into sterilized containers, allowing it to set at room temperature for at least 30 minutes before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bitter orange creams helps with abdominal pain because they contain citrus aurantium, a natural compound that has anti-inflammatory properties. When consumed as an herbal bitter orange cream, this compound can help to reduce inflammation and discomfort in the stomach and digestive tract, providing relief from abdominal pain caused by conditions such as irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) and stomach ulcers. Additionally, the cream's soothing and calming effects can also ease cramps and spasms, promoting a sense of comfort and relaxation.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Herbal bitter orange creams contain compounds like bergamot oil, which can help relax smooth muscle contractions in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramps that contribute to abdominal pain.

Anti-inflammatory property: Bitter orange contains flavonoids like naringenin, which have been shown to reduce inflammation in the body, helping to alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) such as abdominal pain, bloating, and discomfort.

Gastric soothing property: The bitter compounds present in herbal bitter orange creams can stimulate gastric contractions and improve digestion, reducing discomfort and pain associated with indigestion, acid reflux, and other gastrointestinal issues.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and alkaloids, which help to soothe digestive issues and reduce inflammation, making them an effective ingredient in creams for abdominal pain.

Barks: Containing compounds like limonene and citral, the barks of bitter orange have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them suitable for use in creams to alleviate abdominal discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Gather 500ml of cold-pressed sweet almond oil and 100g of dried citrus x aurantium peel.
Grind the citrus x aurantium peel into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 30 seconds.
Mix the ground citrus x aurantium peel with 5% of the total weight in 10ml of jojoba oil for infusion.
Infuse the mixture by placing it in a double boiler at 70-80°c for 2 hours and then straining through cheesecloth.
Combine the infused liquid with the remaining 495ml of sweet almond oil and blend until emulsified.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold creams helps with abdominal pain because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The cream contains antioxidants that reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, alleviating discomfort and cramping. Additionally, the calming effects of pot marigold can help to relax the muscles in the abdomen, reducing spasms and easing digestion. As a result, herbal pot marigold creams provide natural relief from abdominal pain, allowing individuals to find comfort and alleviate symptoms associated with irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), menstrual cramps, and other digestive issues.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Pot marigold creams have anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and terpenes that reduce swelling and ease abdominal pain caused by inflammation.
Antispasmodic property: The cream's antispasmodic properties help relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing cramping and spasms that can cause abdominal pain.
Antioxidant property: Pot marigold's antioxidant compounds neutralize free radicals that can damage tissues and contribute to inflammation and abdominal pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Potent anti-inflammatory compounds in roots help soothe abdominal pain and reduce inflammation.
Seeds: Rich in antioxidants and fatty acids, seeds provide a natural analgesic effect, easing cramps and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried calendula flowers according to local regulations and store them in airtight containers.
Combine 20 grams of dried calendula flowers with 100 milliliters of cold-pressed olive oil in a clean glass jar.
Infuse the mixture for 4 hours under indirect sunlight or low heat stirring occasionally before straining through cheesecloth.
Mix 10 grams of beeswax and 50 milliliters of coconut oil with the calendula-infused oil, then heat gently over low heat until melted.
Pour the cream into sterilized glass jars and let cool to room temperature before sealing and storing in a cool dark place for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Angelica creams helps with abdominal pain because of its natural anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. The herb angelica archangelica contains compounds that relax muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramps that can cause pain. Additionally, angelica's antioxidant activity helps to soothe irritated tissues and reduce inflammation, further alleviating discomfort. As a result, herbal angelica creams provide effective relief from abdominal pain caused by various conditions, including menstrual cramps, IBS, and stomach ulcers.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Angelica creams can help relax smooth muscle spasms in the digestive tract, reducing cramping and discomfort associated with abdominal pain.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The cream's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce inflammation in the abdominal area, which can contribute to relief from conditions like irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) and inflammatory bowel disease (IBD).

Antioxidant properties: Angelica creams' antioxidant activity may help protect the digestive tract lining from oxidative stress and damage, potentially reducing the severity of symptoms such as bloating, gas, and abdominal cramps.
remedy_parts: Roots: Angelica roots are rich in sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them effective in relieving abdominal pain.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzome of the angelica plant is used to treat digestive issues, including abdominal pain, due to its ability to reduce inflammation and improve gut motility.
remedy_recipe: Harvest angelica archangelica roots and leaves, rinse thoroughly with distilled water, and dry in the shade for 2 hours.
Combine 100g of dried angelica archangelica with 500ml of coconut oil in a double boiler, simmering for 4 hours to create a macerate.
Strain the macerate through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container, discarding solids and reserving the infused oil for further processing.
Mix 200g of beeswax with 400ml of the reserved infused oil in a double boiler, heating until melted and well combined, taking approximately 30 minutes to process.
Pour the cooled, creamy mixture into sterilized jars, sealing tightly and storing in the refrigerator for at least 2 weeks before use to allow the cream to mature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender creams helps with abdominal pain because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and discomfort in the digestive tract. The calming essence of lavender also soothes nervous tension and promotes relaxation, alleviating stress-related stomach cramps. Additionally, lavender's analgesic properties help to ease muscle spasms and cramping, providing fast relief from menstrual cramps, IBS symptoms, and other types of abdominal pain.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: English lavender cream's antispasmodic property helps to relax the muscles in the abdominal area, reducing cramps and spasms associated with menstrual cramps, irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), and other digestive issues.

Anti-inflammatory property: The anti-inflammatory compound linalool present in English lavender cream reduces swelling and inflammation in the abdominal area, which can help alleviate symptoms of conditions such as endometriosis, diverticulitis, and Crohn's disease.

Anxiolytic (calming) property: English lavender cream's anxiolytic properties have a calming effect on the nervous system, reducing anxiety and stress that can exacerbate abdominal pain and discomfort associated with conditions like IBS, fibromyalgia, and menstrual cramps.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpenes and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, making them effective in alleviating abdominal pain.
Flowers: Containing linalool and linalyl acetate, these flowers have a calming effect on the digestive system, helping to reduce inflammation and relieve cramps associated with abdominal pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 cups of fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers at dawn when essential oils are most concentrated.
Combine the harvested flowers with 2 cups of carrier oil such as sweet almond or coconut oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 4 hours under direct sunlight to allow for optimal extraction of lavender's anti-inflammatory properties.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth and transfer the infused oil into a double boiler, discarding solids and any remaining liquid.
Whip 1/2 cup of beeswax with 1/2 cup of vitamin e oil and 1/4 cup of the infused oil until smooth and creamy, allowing it to cool and set for at least 30 minutes before use.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for abdominal pain are topical ointments infused with natural herbs that provide relief from stomach discomfort and inflammation. These creams help to ease digestive issues by reducing pain and inflammation, making them an effective alternative to traditional medications. Examples include creams containing ginger, turmeric, and chamomile, which have been shown to provide relief from abdominal pain. This can improve lives by allowing individuals to manage their symptoms and resume daily activities with reduced discomfort.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to abdominal pain that are treated with herbal creams are skin irritations, rashes, and eczema. Herbal creams containing ingredients like aloe vera, chamomile, and calendula soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing inflammation and promoting healing. These natural remedies can also help alleviate symptoms of conditions like acne, psoriasis, and rosacea by reducing redness, itching, and scaling.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with abdominal pain is a blend of Peppermint, Ginger, and Chamomile. Peppermint oil has anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the digestive system, while Ginger's warming effect helps to ease cramps and spasms. Chamomile oil, rich in antioxidants, calms the mind and body, reducing stress and promoting relaxation. Applying these creams topically can provide quick relief and help to alleviate abdominal pain, promoting a sense of comfort and well-being.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Phytotherapy research : PTR, herbal creams can be beneficial for abdominal pain due to their potential anti-acid and gastro-protective properties. The study reviewed the anti-ulcer properties of various herbal medicines, including flavonoids, saponins, tannins, gums, and mucilages, which have been traditionally used by herbalists and indigenous healers for the prevention and treatment of peptic ulcer. Among the herbal drugs with documented clinical efficacy are liquorice, aloe gel, and capsicum (chilli), suggesting that these and similar herbal creams may help alleviate abdominal pain by protecting the stomach lining and reducing inflammation.
